title: Pods, Multi-Container Composition, Lifecycle, Termination, and Debug Containers · Kubernetes Retrieval
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs pods, multi-container composition, lifecycle, termination, and debug containers from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.
Choose init, app, native sidecar, adapter, ambassador, and helper containers from startup, restart, ownership, shutdown, and resource contracts.
Trace waiting, running, terminated, reasons, exit codes, restartPolicy, backoff, previous state, and Pod phase without equating them.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: K8s Pod Shared Boundaries · changed case · Review K8s Pod Shared Boundaries
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 5, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.
